attrs.source: LinkedIn post by Stanislav Beliaev + GitHub repo
attrs.summary: Python CLI qui donne aux agents IA accès au web: X/Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, RSS, pages web. Pas d API payante, setup en une ligne. 16k stars GitHub. Compatible Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf. Commande agent-reach doctor pour vérifier l état.
attrs.action: À tester pour voir si pertinent pour OpenClaw/Jarvis

agent-reach-platforms.label: Plateformes supportées: X, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, RSS, 17+

attrs.summary: 17+ plateformes: X (cookie auth via twitter-cli), Reddit, YouTube (transcripts), GitHub, Bilibili, Weibo, LinkedIn, RSS, pages web classiques. Zero API fees.

Extension du pattern Karpathy: ajouter un Postgres à côté du wiki markdown. Text sources → LLM → Wiki. Data sources → LLM → Database. Les deux couches se référencent mutuellement. Le wiki cite des SQL queries, les tables de données sont interprétées dans des pages wiki. Ni l un ni l autre seul ne suffit pour des décisions business.

Deux compilateurs: texte→wiki (markdown) et données→database (Postgres/SQLite). Les weekly reports (wiki) citent des SQL queries (DB). Les métriques (DB) sont interprétées dans des pages wiki. Knowledge et data se comportent différemment: les concepts se réécrivent, les chiffres s accumulent.

attrs.author: Henrique Bastos
attrs.summary: A harness turns ad hoc processes into loops where cost per iteration approaches zero. XP practices (TDD, CI, refactoring) are harness engineering. AI agents make this personal: fix the environment, not the prompt.

attrs.description: Ad hoc: output doesnt feed input, requires human memory, constant cost, context lost. Loop: output IS next setup, no memory needed, marginal cost drops to zero, context in artifacts.
Pattern works at every level: code (test suites), tasks (skills/commands), workflows (state machines), systems (orchestrators), codebases (docs/linters/standards). Same pattern, different scale.

Every XP practice is a harness-construction technique: TDD, small releases, CI, refactoring, pair programming, collective ownership, coding standards. Named by Kent Beck in 1999, unnamed as meta-pattern until now.

AI agents need XP properties to function. Without them, every session starts from scratch. The cost of not doing XP moved from organizational to personal. Fix the environment not the prompt. Prompt fixes dont compound, environment fixes do.
One entry, one exit, clear boundaries. Non-determinism inside, stable interface outside. Start with one boundary — one process that requires memory. Make it leave the system ready for next run.

Humans between iterations not inside loops. Three roles: Steer (set goals), Evaluate (quality judgment at boundary), Improve the harness (meta-work that compounds). If you carry state between iterations, you reintroduced ad hoc.
Brownfield: use Strangler Fig pattern. Build small loop beside ad hoc system, gradually route more. Never big-bang. Greenfield: start AI-first, build boundaries from day one.
Plan-Do-Check-Act described in 1950s. Toyota built empire on it. Harness is the unnamed infrastructure that makes PDCA actually work.

he_improvement_in_loop.label: Improvement as Part of the Loop

attrs.description: Dont treat improvement as separate phase. Make every iteration generate info about its own performance. Divergences are signals pointing at where to tighten boundaries next.
Clean-after vs clean-before. Clean-after = system always ready for next person = optimize for next iterations time to value. Readiness is emotionally lighter. Near-zero start cost.
Ad hoc process costs the same forever. A loop costs less every iteration. Thats compounding. Environment fixes compound, prompt fixes dont.

attrs.role: Creator of Extreme Programming, published XP Explained in 1999
Quality management pioneer, described PDCA cycle in 1950s

attrs.details: Kent Beck published Extreme Programming Explained. Every practice is a harness-construction technique.

attrs.summary: Architecture for deterministic RAG using 3-tier retrieval: QuadStore knowledge graph (atomic facts) + statistical graph + ChromaDB vector DB, with prompt-enforced conflict resolution instead of algorithmic routing.

attrs.description: RAG architecture that uses knowledge graphs for structured fact retrieval alongside vector databases for semantic search.
Priority 1: absolute graph facts (QuadStore SPOC), Priority 2: statistical/historical graph data, Priority 3: vector documents (ChromaDB). Higher priority overrides lower on conflicts.

Instead of algorithmic routing (like RRF), dump all retrieval results into context with labeled priority blocks and let the LM deterministically resolve conflicts via explicit rules in the system prompt.

Lightweight in-memory knowledge graph using Subject-Predicate-Object-Context format. String-to-integer mapping + 4-way index (spoc/pocs/ocsp/cspo) for constant-time lookups. Simple API: add() and query().
ChromaDB used as Priority 3 fallback for long-tail fuzzy context when knowledge graphs lack the answer.
spaCy NER extracts entities from user prompts to bridge between graph (strict lookup) and vector (semantic similarity) queries.

attrs.stack: Python, QuadStore, ChromaDB, spaCy, Ollama, llama3.2
